extra.reasoning: The input variable `dizziness` does not appear in the premise of any rule (r1 keys on dyspnea, r2 on leg_swelling, r3 on stiff_neck) nor in the default. Therefore the output `acuity` is a pure function of (dyspnea, leg_swelling, stiff_neck) and is completely insensitive to the value of `dizziness`. For any fixed assignment of the other three inputs, flipping dizziness from false to true leaves the set of matching rules, the priority resolution, and the default fallback all unchanged, so acuity_after exactly equals acuity_before. The monotonicity property requires acuity to be no lower after setting dizziness true; equality trivially satisfies "no lower." The acuity computation is also well-defined: the only priority-3 rules (r1, r3) both assign `emergent`, so even when both fire there is no value conflict; r2 (priority 2, urgent) is dominated when a priority-3 rule fires; otherwise default routine. Since the output cannot decrease (in fact cannot change at all) when dizziness is toggled, the property holds across the entire input space and no counterexample exists.
